tuv.seg: The changes in rainfall rates associated with monsoons was reflected in the predicted monthly river plastic inputs into oceans.
Les changements dans les taux de précipitations associés aux moussons se reflétaient dans les prévisions d'apports mensuels de plastique dans les océans via les rivières.

tu.prop: The changes in rainfall rates associated with monsoons was reflected in the predicted monthly river plastic inputs into oceans.
Similarly, our model showed midpoint input estimates from the Ganges River peaking in August with 44,500 tonnes per month while the river discharges <150 tonnes per month between December and March.

tuv.seg: The Yangtze River contributed a midpoint estimate of 76,000 tonnes per month in July but <2,500 tonnes per month in January.
Le fleuve Yangtsé a permis une estimation médiane de 76 000 tonnes par mois en juillet, mais de moins de 2 500 tonnes par mois en janvier.

tu.prop: The Yangtze River contributed a midpoint estimate of 76,000 tonnes per month in July but <2,500 tonnes per month in January.
In Southeast Asia however, maximum river plastic inputs occurred early in the year, during the regional rainy season.

tuv.seg: Similarly, our model showed midpoint input estimates from the Ganges River peaking in August with 44,500 tonnes per month while the river discharges <150 tonnes per month between December and March.

De même, notre modèle a calculé des estimations médianes des apports du Gange culminant en août avec 44 500 tonnes par mois, tandis que le fleuve déverse moins de 150 tonnes par mois entre décembre et mars.

tu.prop: Similarly, our model showed midpoint input estimates from the Ganges River peaking in August with 44,500 tonnes per month while the river discharges <150 tonnes per month between December and March.

In Indonesia for example, modelled inputs of the archipelago reached a midpoint estimate peak in February with 35,000 tonnes per month, as opposed to 1,800 tonnes per month in August during the dry season.

tuv.seg: In Southeast Asia however, maximum river plastic inputs occurred early in the year, during the regional rainy season.
Cependant, en Asie du Sud-Est, les apports de plastique fluvial ont atteint leur maximum au début de l’année, pendant la saison des pluies régionale.

tu.prop: In Southeast Asia however, maximum river plastic inputs occurred early in the year, during the regional rainy season.
Looking at seasonal inputs worldwide, it appears that the relative variations in monthly inputs from the Asian subcontinent are not as pronounced as for other continents (Fig. 3b).

tuv.seg: In Indonesia for example, modelled inputs of the archipelago reached a midpoint estimate peak in February with 35,000 tonnes per month, as opposed to 1,800 tonnes per month in August during the dry season.
En Indonésie par exemple, les apports modélisés de l'archipel ont atteint un pic médian estimé en février avec 35 000 tonnes par mois, contre 1 800 tonnes par mois en août pendant la saison sèche.

tu.prop: In Indonesia for example, modelled inputs of the archipelago reached a midpoint estimate peak in February with 35,000 tonnes per month, as opposed to 1,800 tonnes per month in August during the dry season.

This is caused by a balance between inputs from East Asia and the Indian Subcontinent during the northern hemisphere summer and contributions from Southeast Asia during the southern hemisphere summer.

tuv.seg: Looking at seasonal inputs worldwide, it appears that the relative variations in monthly inputs from the Asian subcontinent are not as pronounced as for other continents (Fig. 3b).

En examinant les apports saisonniers à l’échelle mondiale, il apparaît que les variations relatives des apports mensuels du sous-continent asiatique ne sont pas aussi élevées que pour les autres continents (Données 3b).

tu.prop: Looking at seasonal inputs worldwide, it appears that the relative variations in monthly inputs from the Asian subcontinent are not as pronounced as for other continents (Fig. 3b).

For other parts of the world, our model showed two distinct river plastic input peaks: one occurring between June and October for African, North and Central American rivers, and one occurring from November to May for European, South American and Australia-Pacific rivers.

tuv.seg: This is caused by a balance between inputs from East Asia and the Indian Subcontinent during the northern hemisphere summer and contributions from Southeast Asia during the southern hemisphere summer.
Ceci est dû à un équilibre entre les apports de l’Asie de l’Est et du sous-continent indien pendant l’été de l’hémisphère nord et les apports de l’Asie du Sud-Est pendant l’été de l’hémisphère sud.

tu.prop: This is caused by a balance between inputs from East Asia and the Indian Subcontinent during the northern hemisphere summer and contributions from Southeast Asia during the southern hemisphere summer.

tuv.seg: For other parts of the world, our model showed two distinct river plastic input peaks: one occurring between June and October for African, North and Central American rivers, and one occurring from November to May for European, South American and Australia-Pacific rivers.

Pour d’autres parties du monde, notre modèle a montré deux pics distincts d’apport de plastique dans les rivières : un survenant entre juin et octobre pour les rivières d’Afrique, d’Amérique du Nord et d’Amérique centrale, et un autre survenant de novembre à mai pour les rivières d’Europe, d’Amérique du Sud et d’Australie-Pacifique.

tu.prop: For other parts of the world, our model showed two distinct river plastic input peaks: one occurring between June and October for African, North and Central American rivers, and one occurring from November to May for European, South American and Australia-Pacific rivers.
Here we provide a global estimate of plastic emissions from rivers into the world’s oceans: between 1.15 and 2.41 million tonnes per year.

tuv.seg: Discussion
Discussion

tu.prop: Most of this river plastic input is coming from Asia, which emphasise the need to focus monitoring and mitigation efforts in Asian countries with rapid economic development and poor waste management.

tuv.seg: Here we provide a global estimate of plastic emissions from rivers into the world’s oceans: between 1.15 and 2.41 million tonnes per year.

Nous prévoyons une pollution plastique globale annuelle des océans comprise entre 1,15 et 2,41 millions de tonnes de déchets plastique provenant des rivières avec une contribution dominante des rivières asiatiques.

tu.prop: Here we provide a global estimate of plastic emissions from rivers into the world’s oceans: between 1.15 and 2.41 million tonnes per year.
Research on freshwater plastic pollution is a relatively new field and most efforts have been carried out in industrialized countries of Europe and North America.

tuv.seg: Most of this river plastic input is coming from Asia, which emphasise the need to focus monitoring and mitigation efforts in Asian countries with rapid economic development and poor waste management.

La majeure partie de cet apport de plastique fluvial provient d’Asie, ce qui souligne la nécessité de concentrer les efforts de surveillance et d’atténuation sur les pays asiatiques connaissant un développement économique rapide et une mauvaise gestion des déchets.

tu.prop: Most of this river plastic input is coming from Asia, which emphasise the need to focus monitoring and mitigation efforts in Asian countries with rapid economic development and poor waste management.
While many indicators suggest a dominant contribution of plastics from Asian countries20, there is very little data to document these assumptions and thoroughly verify the validity of our model.

tuv.seg: Research on freshwater plastic pollution is a relatively new field and most efforts have been carried out in industrialized countries of Europe and North America.
La recherche sur la pollution plastique des eaux douces est un domaine relativement nouveau et la plupart des efforts ont été menés dans les pays industrialisés d’Europe et d’Amérique du Nord.

tu.prop: Research on freshwater plastic pollution is a relatively new field and most efforts have been carried out in industrialized countries of Europe and North America.

Yet, the relatively high concentrations of ocean plastic found at the surface of the North Pacific Ocean28,29 where buoyant plastics originating from Asia can accumulate30, suggest that our assumptions are plausible.

tuv.seg: While many indicators suggest a dominant contribution of plastics from Asian countries20, there is very little data to document these assumptions and thoroughly verify the validity of our model.

Si de nombreux indicateurs suggèrent une contribution dominante des plastiques en provenance des pays asiatiques[20], il existe très peu de données pour documenter ces hypothèses et vérifier minutieusement la validité de notre modèle.

tu.prop: While many indicators suggest a dominant contribution of plastics from Asian countries20, there is very little data to document these assumptions and thoroughly verify the validity of our model.
It has been estimated that between 4.8 and 12.7 million tonnes of plastic enters the ocean every year from coastal populations worldwide5.

tuv.seg: Yet, the relatively high concentrations of ocean plastic found at the surface of the North Pacific Ocean28,29 where buoyant plastics originating from Asia can accumulate30, suggest that our assumptions are plausible.

Pourtant, les concentrations relativement élevées de plastique océanique relevées à la surface de l’océan Pacifique Nord[28],[29], où peuvent s’accumuler des plastiques flottants provenant d’Asie[30], suggèrent que nos hypothèses sont plausibles.

tu.prop: Yet, the relatively high concentrations of ocean plastic found at the surface of the North Pacific Ocean28,29 where buoyant plastics originating from Asia can accumulate30, suggest that our assumptions are plausible.
Plastics within coastal areas however do not only enter the oceans through rivers.

tuv.seg: It has been estimated that between 4.8 and 12.7 million tonnes of plastic enters the ocean every year from coastal populations worldwide5.
On estime qu’entre 4,8 et 12,7 millions de tonnes de plastique provenant des populations côtières du monde entier pénètrent dans l’océan chaque année[5].

tu.prop: It has been estimated that between 4.8 and 12.7 million tonnes of plastic enters the ocean every year from coastal populations worldwide5.
It can also reach oceans by other processes such as direct littering near beaches, followed by tidal or wind transport4.

tuv.seg: Plastics within coastal areas however do not only enter the oceans through rivers.
Toutefois, les plastiques présents dans les zones côtières ne pénètrent pas dans les océans uniquement par les rivières.

tu.prop: Plastics within coastal areas however do not only enter the oceans through rivers.

It is also important to note that our model is calibrated against buoyant plastics found on river surface waters, whereas this previous coastal contribution assessment considers all types of plastics found in municipal waste.

tuv.seg: It can also reach oceans by other processes such as direct littering near beaches, followed by tidal or wind transport4.
Ils peuvent également atteindre les océans par d’autres processus tels que le rejet direct des déchets près des plages, suivi du transport par les marées ou le vent[4].

tu.prop: It can also reach oceans by other processes such as direct littering near beaches, followed by tidal or wind transport4.

Finally, we only consider a section of the full plastic debris size spectrum as particles smaller than the mesh size of the sampling nets were not accounted for and debris larger than the aperture size of the trawl devices are under-represented.

tuv.seg: It is also important to note that our model is calibrated against buoyant plastics found on river surface waters, whereas this previous coastal contribution assessment considers all types of plastics found in municipal waste.

Il est également important de noter que notre modèle est calibré sur les plastiques flottants trouvés dans les eaux de surface des rivières, alors que la précédente évaluation de la contribution côtière prend en compte tous les types de plastiques trouvés dans les déchets municipaux.

tu.prop: It is also important to note that our model is calibrated against buoyant plastics found on river surface waters, whereas this previous coastal contribution assessment considers all types of plastics found in municipal waste.
For these reasons our estimate should be considered conservative.

tuv.seg: Finally, we only consider a section of the full plastic debris size spectrum as particles smaller than the mesh size of the sampling nets were not accounted for and debris larger than the aperture size of the trawl devices are under-represented.

Les études utilisées pour cette évaluation ne couvrent qu'une fraction du large spectre de taille des débris plastiques : les particules plus petites que la taille des mailles des filets d’échantillonnage n’ont pas été prises en compte et les débris plus grands que la taille de l’ouverture des chaluts sont sous-représentés.

tu.prop: Finally, we only consider a section of the full plastic debris size spectrum as particles smaller than the mesh size of the sampling nets were not accounted for and debris larger than the aperture size of the trawl devices are under-represented.

Nonetheless, for comparison purposes, we applied our model to calculate the contribution of coastal populations (that is, living within 50 km from the coastline) and obtained a global contribution from coastal population of 356,000–893,000 tonnes per year.

tuv.seg: For these reasons our estimate should be considered conservative.
Pour ces raisons, notre estimation doit être utilisée avec prudence.

tu.prop: For these reasons our estimate should be considered conservative.
This suggests that at least 2.8–18.6% of the coastal plastic emissions occur via river transport.

tuv.seg: Nonetheless, for comparison purposes, we applied our model to calculate the contribution of coastal populations (that is, living within 50 km from the coastline) and obtained a global contribution from coastal population of 356,000–893,000 tonnes per year.

Néanmoins, à des fins de comparaison, nous avons appliqué notre modèle au calcul de la contribution des populations côtières (c'est-à-dire vivant à moins de 50 km du littoral) et avons obtenu une contribution globale de la population côtière de 356 000 à 893 000 tonnes par an.

tu.prop: Nonetheless, for comparison purposes, we applied our model to calculate the contribution of coastal populations (that is, living within 50 km from the coastline) and obtained a global contribution from coastal population of 356,000–893,000 tonnes per year.
Furthermore, our findings indicate that an additional plastic input of at least 0.79–1.52 million tonnes per year reaches oceans from inland areas via rivers.

tuv.seg: This suggests that at least 2.8–18.6% of the coastal plastic emissions occur via river transport.
Cela implique qu’au moins 2,8 à 18,6 % des émissions de plastique côtières se produisent via le transport fluvial.

tu.prop: This suggests that at least 2.8–18.6% of the coastal plastic emissions occur via river transport.
It should be stressed that there is a great level of uncertainty related to both measurements and models of plastic transport in riverine systems.

tuv.seg: Furthermore, our findings indicate that an additional plastic input of at least 0.79–1.52 million tonnes per year reaches oceans from inland areas via rivers.
En outre, nos résultats indiquent qu’un apport supplémentaire de plastique d’au moins 0,79 à 1,52 million de tonnes par an atteint les océans depuis les zones intérieures via les rivières.

tu.prop: Furthermore, our findings indicate that an additional plastic input of at least 0.79–1.52 million tonnes per year reaches oceans from inland areas via rivers.
Plastic sources, deposition and degradation processes are poorly understood, and may play an important role in the size and spatial distributions of plastic in freshwater ecosystems31.

tuv.seg: It should be stressed that there is a great level of uncertainty related to both measurements and models of plastic transport in riverine systems.
Il convient de souligner qu’il existe un grand niveau d’incertitude lié à la fois aux mesures et aux modèles de transport du plastique dans les systèmes fluviaux.

tu.prop: It should be stressed that there is a great level of uncertainty related to both measurements and models of plastic transport in riverine systems.

For instance, when taking into account the uncertainties related to the extrapolations we made in the field observation dataset, our global input prediction doubled from lower to upper estimates (Supplementary Table 1).

tuv.seg: Plastic sources, deposition and degradation processes are poorly understood, and may play an important role in the size and spatial distributions of plastic in freshwater ecosystems31.

Les sources d'émission de plastique, ainsi que les processus de dépôt et de dégradation sont mal compris et peuvent jouer un rôle important dans la taille et la répartition spatiale du plastique dans les écosystèmes d'eau douce[31].

tu.prop: Plastic sources, deposition and degradation processes are poorly understood, and may play an important role in the size and spatial distributions of plastic in freshwater ecosystems31.
Furthermore, a major assumption of our model is that artificial barriers such as dams act as sinks for all plastic particles.

tuv.seg: For instance, when taking into account the uncertainties related to the extrapolations we made in the field observation dataset, our global input prediction doubled from lower to upper estimates (Supplementary Table 1).

Par exemple, en tenant compte des incertitudes liées aux extrapolations que nous avons effectuées dans l'ensemble de données d'observation sur le terrain, notre prévision d'entrée globale a doublé, passant des estimations inférieures aux estimations supérieures (tableau complémentaire 1).

tu.prop: For instance, when taking into account the uncertainties related to the extrapolations we made in the field observation dataset, our global input prediction doubled from lower to upper estimates (Supplementary Table 1).
This is a conservative assumption since sediment trap efficiency of reservoirs may vary upon several factors32.

tuv.seg: Furthermore, a major assumption of our model is that artificial barriers such as dams act as sinks for all plastic particles.
En outre, une hypothèse majeure de notre modèle est que les barrières artificielles telles que les barrages agissent comme des puits pour toutes les particules de plastique.

tu.prop: Furthermore, a major assumption of our model is that artificial barriers such as dams act as sinks for all plastic particles.

There is a need for more extensive monitoring of freshwater contamination by plastic pollution with systematic samplings in sediment and surface waters of rivers throughout the year to better understand seasonality of inputs.

tuv.seg: This is a conservative assumption since sediment trap efficiency of reservoirs may vary upon several factors32.
Il s’agit d’une hypothèse prudente puisque l’efficacité des pièges à sédiments des réservoirs peut varier en fonction de plusieurs facteurs[32].

tu.prop: This is a conservative assumption since sediment trap efficiency of reservoirs may vary upon several factors32.
Methodologies and reported units should be better standardized, as these vary widely across assessments.

tuv.seg: There is a need for more extensive monitoring of freshwater contamination by plastic pollution with systematic samplings in sediment and surface waters of rivers throughout the year to better understand seasonality of inputs.

Il est nécessaire d'effectuer une surveillance plus approfondie de la contamination des eaux douces par la pollution plastique avec des échantillonnages systématiques des sédiments et des eaux de surface des rivières tout au long de l'année pour mieux comprendre la saisonnalité des apports.

tu.prop: There is a need for more extensive monitoring of freshwater contamination by plastic pollution with systematic samplings in sediment and surface waters of rivers throughout the year to better understand seasonality of inputs.
Furthermore, plastics should not be regarded as a generic material but must be classified into classes (for example, polymer types, debris sizes).

tuv.seg: Methodologies and reported units should be better standardized, as these vary widely across assessments.
Les méthodologies et les unités déclarées devraient être mieux standardisées, car elles varient considérablement d'une évaluation à l'autre.

tu.prop: Methodologies and reported units should be better standardized, as these vary widely across assessments.

Defining better standards for monitoring plastic pollution in freshwater ecosystems would allow more robust comparisons across catchments and higher levels of sophistication of numerical models at global scale.

tuv.seg: Furthermore, plastics should not be regarded as a generic material but must be classified into classes (for example, polymer types, debris sizes).
En outre, les plastiques ne doivent pas être considérés comme un matériau générique mais doivent être classés en classes (par exemple, types de polymères, tailles de débris).

tu.prop: Furthermore, plastics should not be regarded as a generic material but must be classified into classes (for example, polymer types, debris sizes).
Long-term monitoring would improve the definition of the sampled debris size spectrum.

tuv.seg: Defining better standards for monitoring plastic pollution in freshwater ecosystems would allow more robust comparisons across catchments and higher levels of sophistication of numerical models at global scale.

Définir de meilleures normes concernant la surveillance de la pollution plastique dans les écosystèmes d’eau douce permettrait des comparaisons plus solides entre les bassins versants et une plus haute sophistication des modèles numériques à l’échelle mondiale.

tu.prop: Defining better standards for monitoring plastic pollution in freshwater ecosystems would allow more robust comparisons across catchments and higher levels of sophistication of numerical models at global scale.
Studies should aim at providing mass estimates and not only focus on reporting numbers of particles per units of volume (or surface area).

tuv.seg: Long-term monitoring would improve the definition of the sampled debris size spectrum.
Une surveillance à long terme améliorerait la définition du spectre de taille des débris échantillonnés.

tu.prop: Long-term monitoring would improve the definition of the sampled debris size spectrum.
Reporting weight is critical to assess conservative budget assessment and compare estimates against global plastic production statistics.

tuv.seg: Studies should aim at providing mass estimates and not only focus on reporting numbers of particles per units of volume (or surface area).
Les études devraient viser à fournir des estimations de masse et ne pas se concentrer uniquement sur le nombre de particules par unité de volume (ou de surface).

tu.prop: Studies should aim at providing mass estimates and not only focus on reporting numbers of particles per units of volume (or surface area).

Finally, depending on precipitation patterns, soil characteristics25 and river hydrodynamics31, micro- and macro-plastic retention, as well as vertical distribution may vary significantly along rivers.

tuv.seg: Reporting weight is critical to assess conservative budget assessment and compare estimates against global plastic production statistics.
La pondération des rapports est essentielle pour faire une évaluation budgétaire conservatrice et comparer les estimations aux statistiques mondiales sur la production de plastique.

tu.prop: Reporting weight is critical to assess conservative budget assessment and compare estimates against global plastic production statistics.

Similarly, to open ocean waters where empirical models using sea surface wind velocities, sea state and particles properties have been proposed33,34,35, an approach predicting plastic loads in the water column of rivers as a function of its physical and geological characteristics would be beneficial to account for suspended particles and refine our estimates.

tuv.seg: Finally, depending on precipitation patterns, soil characteristics25 and river hydrodynamics31, micro- and macro-plastic retention, as well as vertical distribution may vary significantly along rivers.

Enfin, en fonction des régimes de précipitations, des caractéristiques du sol et de l’hydrodynamique des rivières, la rétention micro et macroplastique ainsi que la répartition verticale peuvent considérablement varier le long des rivières.

tu.prop: Finally, depending on precipitation patterns, soil characteristics25 and river hydrodynamics31, micro- and macro-plastic retention, as well as vertical distribution may vary significantly along rivers.
Here we provide a geographical and seasonal distribution of plastic inputs from rivers into the ocean at global scale.

tuv.seg: Similarly, to open ocean waters where empirical models using sea surface wind velocities, sea state and particles properties have been proposed33,34,35, an approach predicting plastic loads in the water column of rivers as a function of its physical and geological characteristics would be beneficial to account for suspended particles and refine our estimates.

De même, pour les eaux océaniques ouvertes où des modèles empiriques utilisant la vitesse du vent à la surface de la mer, l'état de la mer et les propriétés des particules ont été proposés, une approche prédisant les charges plastiques dans la colonne d'eau des rivières en fonction de ses caractéristiques physiques et géologiques serait bénéfique pour prendre en compte les particules en suspension et affiner nos estimations.

tu.prop: Similarly, to open ocean waters where empirical models using sea surface wind velocities, sea state and particles properties have been proposed33,34,35, an approach predicting plastic loads in the water column of rivers as a function of its physical and geological characteristics would be beneficial to account for suspended particles and refine our estimates.
Both our methodology and model predictions can be refined once more, data are made available.

tuv.seg: Here we provide a geographical and seasonal distribution of plastic inputs from rivers into the ocean at global scale.
Nous fournissons ici une répartition géographique et saisonnière des apports de plastique des rivières vers l'océan à l'échelle mondiale.

tu.prop: Here we provide a geographical and seasonal distribution of plastic inputs from rivers into the ocean at global scale.
While population density and waste management data demonstrate a good correlation with results from observational river studies, freshwater plastic contamination can occur from different sources.

tuv.seg: Both our methodology and model predictions can be refined once more, data are made available.
Notre méthodologie et nos prévisions de modèles peuvent être à nouveau affinées, les données sont mises à disposition.

tu.prop: Both our methodology and model predictions can be refined once more, data are made available.
For example, micro-plastics retained in waste water treatment plants can be reintroduced in the environment with application of sewage sludge on agricultural lands36.

tuv.seg: While population density and waste management data demonstrate a good correlation with results from observational river studies, freshwater plastic contamination can occur from different sources.

Bien que les données sur la densité de population et la gestion des déchets démontrent une bonne corrélation avec les résultats des études observationnelles sur les rivières, la contamination des eaux douces par les plastiques peut provenir de différentes sources.

tu.prop: While population density and waste management data demonstrate a good correlation with results from observational river studies, freshwater plastic contamination can occur from different sources.

As such, our model could include multiple source scenarios such as land use characteristics and economic activities to account for the inputs from the industrial and agricultural sectors, land transportation, construction, tourism, fisheries and aquaculture industries.

tuv.seg: For example, micro-plastics retained in waste water treatment plants can be reintroduced in the environment with application of sewage sludge on agricultural lands36.
Par exemple, les microplastiques retenus dans les stations d’épuration des eaux usées peuvent être réintroduits dans l’environnement avec l’épandage de boues d’épuration sur les terres agricoles[36].

tu.prop: For example, micro-plastics retained in waste water treatment plants can be reintroduced in the environment with application of sewage sludge on agricultural lands36.

The modelling of local sinks for plastics on rivers could also be refined with the integration of river morphologies and local hydrodynamics (for example, sedimentation, remobilization), as well as the presence of natural (for example, mangrove) and artificial (for example, river groynes or waste water treatment plants) features.

tuv.seg: As such, our model could include multiple source scenarios such as land use characteristics and economic activities to account for the inputs from the industrial and agricultural sectors, land transportation, construction, tourism, fisheries and aquaculture industries.

En tant que tel, notre modèle pourrait inclure plusieurs scénarios de sources tels que les caractéristiques d'utilisation des terres et les activités économiques pour tenir compte des intrants des secteurs industriel et agricole, du transport terrestre, de la construction, du tourisme, de la pêche et de l'aquaculture.

tu.prop: As such, our model could include multiple source scenarios such as land use characteristics and economic activities to account for the inputs from the industrial and agricultural sectors, land transportation, construction, tourism, fisheries and aquaculture industries.
Our results are made available in Figshare37 in an attempt to assist researchers and policy makers with selecting locations for future monitoring and mitigation efforts.

tuv.seg: The modelling of local sinks for plastics on rivers could also be refined with the integration of river morphologies and local hydrodynamics (for example, sedimentation, remobilization), as well as the presence of natural (for example, mangrove) and artificial (for example, river groynes or waste water treatment plants) features.

La modélisation des puits locaux de plastiques sur les rivières pourrait également être affinée avec l'intégration des morphologies des rivières et de l'hydrodynamique locale (par exemple, sédimentation, remobilisation), ainsi que de la présence d'éléments naturels (par exemple, mangrove) et artificiels (par exemple, épis de rivière ou stations d'épuration des eaux usées).

tu.prop: The modelling of local sinks for plastics on rivers could also be refined with the integration of river morphologies and local hydrodynamics (for example, sedimentation, remobilization), as well as the presence of natural (for example, mangrove) and artificial (for example, river groynes or waste water treatment plants) features.

tuv.seg: Our results are made available in Figshare37 in an attempt to assist researchers and policy makers with selecting locations for future monitoring and mitigation efforts.

Nos résultats sont disponibles dans la Fig.37 dans le but d'aider les chercheurs et les décideurs politiques à sélectionner les emplacements pour privilégier les futurs essais de surveillance et d'atténuation.

tu.prop: Our results are made available in Figshare37 in an attempt to assist researchers and policy makers with selecting locations for future monitoring and mitigation efforts.

tuv.seg: Methods
Méthodes

tu.prop: Our global model computes plastic load inputs from 40,760 watersheds worldwide21 into the ocean using geospatial data on population density18,19 and MPW production per inhabitant and per day in 182 individual countries5,20.

tuv.seg: Model approach
Approche par modèle d'apprentissage

tu.prop: Waste is considered mismanaged when it is littered or inadequately disposed.

tuv.seg: Our global model computes plastic load inputs from 40,760 watersheds worldwide21 into the ocean using geospatial data on population density18,19 and MPW production per inhabitant and per day in 182 individual countries5,20.

Notre modèle mondial calcule les apports de charges plastiques provenant de 40 760 bassins versants du monde entier [21] dans l’océan à l’aide de données géospatiales sur la densité de population [18,19] et la production de MPW par habitant et par jour dans 182 pays [5],[20] .

tu.prop: Our global model computes plastic load inputs from 40,760 watersheds worldwide21 into the ocean using geospatial data on population density18,19 and MPW production per inhabitant and per day in 182 individual countries5,20.
MPW corresponds to the fraction of plastic found in mismanaged waste material on land.

tuv.seg: Waste is considered mismanaged when it is littered or inadequately disposed.
Les déchets sont considérés comme mal gérés lorsqu’ils sont jetés ou mal éliminés.

tu.prop: Waste is considered mismanaged when it is littered or inadequately disposed.
This definition was applied in a previous estimate of global plastic waste inputs into the ocean from coastal population worldwide5.

tuv.seg: MPW corresponds to the fraction of plastic found in mismanaged waste material on land.
Les MPW correspondent à la fraction de plastique présente dans les déchets terrestres mal gérés.

tu.prop: MPW corresponds to the fraction of plastic found in mismanaged waste material on land.
MPW production rates are integrated inside catchments.

tuv.seg: This definition was applied in a previous estimate of global plastic waste inputs into the ocean from coastal population worldwide5.
Cette définition a été appliquée dans une précédente évaluation des apports mondiaux de déchets plastiques dans l’océan provenant des populations côtières du monde entier[5].

tu.prop: This definition was applied in a previous estimate of global plastic waste inputs into the ocean from coastal population worldwide5.

The resulting mass is accumulated following natural drainage patterns derived from local topography, and taking into account the presence of artificial barriers (for example, dams and weirs) acting as sinks.

tuv.seg: MPW production rates are integrated inside catchments.
Les taux de production de MPW sont intégrés à l’intérieur des bassins versants.

tu.prop: MPW production rates are integrated inside catchments.
The seasonality of inputs at the outfall location is derived from monthly average catchment runoff.

tuv.seg: The resulting mass is accumulated following natural drainage patterns derived from local topography, and taking into account the presence of artificial barriers (for example, dams and weirs) acting as sinks.

La masse résultante s'accumule en suivant les schémas de drainage naturels dérivés de la topographie locale, et en tenant compte de la présence de barrières artificielles (par exemple, barrages et déversoirs) agissant comme des puits à particules plastiques.

tu.prop: The resulting mass is accumulated following natural drainage patterns derived from local topography, and taking into account the presence of artificial barriers (for example, dams and weirs) acting as sinks.
An empirical relation using integrated MPW mass production upstream of river mouths and seasonal runoff is formulated and calibrated using a set of field observations (Fig. 4).

tuv.seg: The seasonality of inputs at the outfall location is derived from monthly average catchment runoff.
La saisonnalité des apports vers l'emplacement du déversoir est dérivée du ruissellement mensuel moyen du bassin versant.

tu.prop: The seasonality of inputs at the outfall location is derived from monthly average catchment runoff.

tuv.seg: An empirical relation using integrated MPW mass production upstream of river mouths and seasonal runoff is formulated and calibrated using a set of field observations (Fig. 4).

Une relation empirique utilisant la production de masse intégrée de MPW en amont des embouchures des rivières et le ruissellement saisonnier est formulée et calibrée à l'aide d'un ensemble d'observations sur le terrain (Fig. 4).

tu.prop: An empirical relation using integrated MPW mass production upstream of river mouths and seasonal runoff is formulated and calibrated using a set of field observations (Fig. 4).

tuv.seg: Figure 4: Model framework.
Figure 4 : Modèle de cadre

tu.prop: Plastic mass production per river catchment (Mmpw; n=40,760 rivers) is computed from data on MPW production rates per country, population density, topographic elevation and location of artificial barriers.

figure 4

tu.prop: Seasonality of inputs is derived from monthly averaged runoff (R).

tuv.seg: Plastic mass production per river catchment (Mmpw; n=40,760 rivers) is computed from data on MPW production rates per country, population density, topographic elevation and location of artificial barriers.

La production massive de plastique par bassin versant (Mmpw ; n = 40 760 rivières) est calculée à partir de données sur les taux de production de MPW par pays, la densité de population, l'altitude topographique ainsi que de l'emplacement des barrières artificielles.

tu.prop: Plastic mass production per river catchment (Mmpw; n=40,760 rivers) is computed from data on MPW production rates per country, population density, topographic elevation and location of artificial barriers.
A parametric equation with parameters k and a is used to fit model predictions (Mout) against results from observational studies.

tuv.seg: Seasonality of inputs is derived from monthly averaged runoff (R).
La saisonnalité des apports est dérivée du ruissellement mensuel moyen (R).

tu.prop: Seasonality of inputs is derived from monthly averaged runoff (R).
For our mid-point estimate, best fit was found for k=1.85 10−3 and a=1.52 (r2=0.93, n=30).

tuv.seg: A parametric equation with parameters k and a is used to fit model predictions (Mout) against results from observational studies.
Une équation paramétrique, avec les paramètres k et a, est utilisée pour ajuster les prédictions du modèle (Mout) comparées aux résultats des études observationnelles.

tu.prop: A parametric equation with parameters k and a is used to fit model predictions (Mout) against results from observational studies.

tuv.seg: For our mid-point estimate, best fit was found for k=1.85 10−3 and a=1.52 (r2=0.93, n=30).
Pour notre estimation médiane, le meilleur ajustement a été trouvé pour k = 1,85 · 10−3 et a = 1,52 (r2 = 0,93, n = 30).

tu.prop: For our mid-point estimate, best fit was found for k=1.85 10−3 and a=1.52 (r2=0.93, n=30).

We find a strong coefficient of determination (r2=0.93) for k=1.85 10–3 and a=1.52 (midpoint estimate, Fig. 2) using n=30 records from 13 different rivers, where data on plastic contamination in surface waters were reported in the literature.

tuv.seg: where Mout is the plastic mass release at the outflow in kilogram per day, Mmpw, the mass of MPW produced inside the catchment downstream of artificial barriers and R, monthly averaged catchment runoff. k and a are the regression parameters.

dans laquelle Mout représente la masse de plastique rejetée à la sortie en kilogrammes par jour, Mmpw, la masse de MPW produite à l'intérieur du bassin versant en aval des barrières artificielles et R, le ruissellement moyen mensuel du bassin versant. k et a sont les paramètres de régression.

tu.prop: where Mout is the plastic mass release at the outflow in kilogram per day, Mmpw, the mass of MPW produced inside the catchment downstream of artificial barriers and R, monthly averaged catchment runoff. k and a are the regression parameters.

We considered only peer-reviewed studies that provided reliable estimates of plastic concentrations (number and/or mass of plastic particles per volume and/or area of river water) using surface net devices.

tuv.seg: We find a strong coefficient of determination (r2=0.93) for k=1.85 10–3 and a=1.52 (midpoint estimate, Fig. 2) using n=30 records from 13 different rivers, where data on plastic contamination in surface waters were reported in the literature.

Nous trouvons un fort coefficient de détermination (r2 = 0,93) pour k = 1,85 10–3 et a = 1,52 (estimation médiane, Fig. 2) en utilisant n = 30 enregistrements de 13 rivières différentes, où les données sur la contamination plastique dans les eaux de surface ont été rapportées dans la littérature.

tu.prop: We find a strong coefficient of determination (r2=0.93) for k=1.85 10–3 and a=1.52 (midpoint estimate, Fig. 2) using n=30 records from 13 different rivers, where data on plastic contamination in surface waters were reported in the literature.

For plastic concentrations reported in number of particles per unit area of river surface, we used the depth of the trawling devices to convert reported surface areas (km2) into volume of water sampled (m3).

tuv.seg: We considered only peer-reviewed studies that provided reliable estimates of plastic concentrations (number and/or mass of plastic particles per volume and/or area of river water) using surface net devices.

Nous avons pris en compte uniquement les études évaluées par des pairs qui fournissaient des estimations fiables des concentrations de plastique (nombre et/ou masse de particules de plastique par volume et/ou superficie d'eau de rivière) à l'aide de filets de surface.

tu.prop: We considered only peer-reviewed studies that provided reliable estimates of plastic concentrations (number and/or mass of plastic particles per volume and/or area of river water) using surface net devices.
The bibliographic review and selection criteria described above led to the consideration of seven studies in our model calibration exercise.

tuv.seg: For plastic concentrations reported in number of particles per unit area of river surface, we used the depth of the trawling devices to convert reported surface areas (km2) into volume of water sampled (m3).

Pour les concentrations de plastique rapportées en nombre de particules par unité de surface de surface de la rivière, nous avons utilisé la profondeur des dispositifs de chalutage afin de convertir les surfaces déclarées (km2) en volume d'eau échantillonné (m3).

tu.prop: For plastic concentrations reported in number of particles per unit area of river surface, we used the depth of the trawling devices to convert reported surface areas (km2) into volume of water sampled (m3).
These studies reported river plastic concentrations in 13 rivers, at 30 sampling events that occurred in different time periods (Table 2).

tuv.seg: The bibliographic review and selection criteria described above led to the consideration of seven studies in our model calibration exercise.
L'analyse bibliographique et les critères de sélection décrits ci-dessus ont conduit à la prise en compte de sept études dans notre exercice de calibrage du modèle.

tu.prop: The bibliographic review and selection criteria described above led to the consideration of seven studies in our model calibration exercise.
Our approach is conservative because we neglected the contribution of buoyant plastic that may occur below the sampled depth due to water turbulence13.

tuv.seg: These studies reported river plastic concentrations in 13 rivers, at 30 sampling events that occurred in different time periods (Table 2).
Ces études ont rapporté les concentrations de plastique de 13 rivières, lors de 30 échantillonnages survenus à différentes périodes (tableau 2).

tu.prop: These studies reported river plastic concentrations in 13 rivers, at 30 sampling events that occurred in different time periods (Table 2).

Furthermore, this approach does not account for the contribution of non-buoyant plastics that, once introduced in rivers, may slowly make its way to the oceans due to turbulent transport, accumulating in deep sea river canyons38.

tuv.seg: Our approach is conservative because we neglected the contribution of buoyant plastic that may occur below the sampled depth due to water turbulence13.
Notre approche est conservatrice car nous avons négligé la contribution du plastique flottant qui peut se produire en dessous de la profondeur échantillonnée en raison de la turbulence de l'eau.

tu.prop: Our approach is conservative because we neglected the contribution of buoyant plastic that may occur below the sampled depth due to water turbulence13.

Around 48% of the plastic produced yearly is made of polymers lighter than seawater (Polyethylene and Polypropylene;1), this number is likely higher due to existence of objects made of polymers heavier than seawater that can float due to air entrapment (for example, PET bottles and foamed Polystyrene).

tuv.seg: Furthermore, this approach does not account for the contribution of non-buoyant plastics that, once introduced in rivers, may slowly make its way to the oceans due to turbulent transport, accumulating in deep sea river canyons38.

De plus, cette approche ne tient pas compte de la contribution des plastiques non-flottants qui, une fois introduits dans les rivières, peuvent lentement se frayer un chemin vers les océans en raison du transport turbulent, s'accumulant dans les canyons des rivières profondes.

tu.prop: Furthermore, this approach does not account for the contribution of non-buoyant plastics that, once introduced in rivers, may slowly make its way to the oceans due to turbulent transport, accumulating in deep sea river canyons38.
Not all studies considered here reported micro- and macro-plastic concentrations at surface waters of rivers.

tuv.seg: Around 48% of the plastic produced yearly is made of polymers lighter than seawater (Polyethylene and Polypropylene;1), this number is likely higher due to existence of objects made of polymers heavier than seawater that can float due to air entrapment (for example, PET bottles and foamed Polystyrene).

Environ 48 % du plastique produit chaque année est constitué de polymères plus légers que l'eau de mer (polyéthylène et polypropylène ;1), ce chiffre est probablement plus élevé en raison de l'existence d'objets fabriqués à partir de polymères plus lourds que l'eau de mer qui peuvent flotter en raison de l'emprisonnement d'air (par exemple, les bouteilles en PET et la mousse de polystyrène).

tu.prop: Around 48% of the plastic produced yearly is made of polymers lighter than seawater (Polyethylene and Polypropylene;1), this number is likely higher due to existence of objects made of polymers heavier than seawater that can float due to air entrapment (for example, PET bottles and foamed Polystyrene).
As such, for our midpoint estimate, we homogenized our data set using the mean ratio micro- to macro-plastic numerical concentration from studies reporting both types (mean ratio equals to 0.04).

tuv.seg: Not all studies considered here reported micro- and macro-plastic concentrations at surface waters of rivers.
Les études examinées ici n'ont pas toutes fait état de concentrations de micro- et macroplastiques dans les eaux de surface des rivières.

tu.prop: Not all studies considered here reported micro- and macro-plastic concentrations at surface waters of rivers.
For comparison, a study compiling thousands of samples at sea found a relatively similar mean ratio of 0.07 (ref.

tuv.seg: As such, for our midpoint estimate, we homogenized our data set using the mean ratio micro- to macro-plastic numerical concentration from studies reporting both types (mean ratio equals to 0.04).

En tant que tel, pour notre estimation médiane, nous avons homogénéisé notre ensemble de données en utilisant le rapport moyen des concentrations numériques en micro- et macroplastiques provenant d'études rapportant les deux types (rapport moyen égal à 0,04).

tu.prop: As such, for our midpoint estimate, we homogenized our data set using the mean ratio micro- to macro-plastic numerical concentration from studies reporting both types (mean ratio equals to 0.04).

tuv.seg: For comparison, a study compiling thousands of samples at sea found a relatively similar mean ratio of 0.07 (ref.
A titre de comparaison, une étude compilant des milliers d’échantillons en mer a trouvé un rapport moyen relativement similaire de 0,07 (réf.

tu.prop: For comparison, a study compiling thousands of samples at sea found a relatively similar mean ratio of 0.07 (ref.
When only numerical concentrations were reported, we estimated the mass concentrations using the average mass of micro- and macro-plastic particles sampled at sea: 0.003 and 0.17 g, respectively29.
The results of the standardization exercise are presented in Table 3.

tuv.seg: When only numerical concentrations were reported, we estimated the mass concentrations using the average mass of micro- and macro-plastic particles sampled at sea: 0.003 and 0.17 g, respectively29.

Lorsque seules les concentrations numériques étaient rapportées, nous avons estimé les concentrations massiques en utilisant la masse moyenne des particules micro et macroplastiques échantillonnées en mer : 0,003 et 0,17 g, respectivement.

tu.prop: When only numerical concentrations were reported, we estimated the mass concentrations using the average mass of micro- and macro-plastic particles sampled at sea: 0.003 and 0.17 g, respectively29.

We acknowledge however, that the extrapolations described above are a limitation of the calibration exercise presented here, as the average mass of river plastic particles, as well as the ratio between micro- and macro-plastic concentrations may vary across catchments due to local differences in in-situ fragmentation rates, plastic transport processes and levels of primary micro-plastic emissions (for example, pre-production pellets, microbeads from cosmetics and hygienic products, laundry powders, paint and coating flakes).

tuv.seg: The results of the standardization exercise are presented in Table 3.
Les résultats de l’exercice de normalisation sont présentés dans le tableau 3.

tu.prop: The results of the standardization exercise are presented in Table 3.

A sensitivity analysis was conducted by varying the mean ratio micro- to macro-plastic numerical concentration (range: 0.01–0.12) and the average mass of particles (range: 0.002–0.004 g and 0.04–0.33 g for micro- and macro-plastic, respectively) using range values found at sea29.

tuv.seg: We acknowledge however, that the extrapolations described above are a limitation of the calibration exercise presented here, as the average mass of river plastic particles, as well as the ratio between micro- and macro-plastic concentrations may vary across catchments due to local differences in in-situ fragmentation rates, plastic transport processes and levels of primary micro-plastic emissions (for example, pre-production pellets, microbeads from cosmetics and hygienic products, laundry powders, paint and coating flakes).

Nous reconnaissons cependant que les extrapolations décrites ci-dessus constituent une limitation de l'exercice d'étalonnage présenté ici, car la masse moyenne des particules plastiques des rivières, ainsi que le rapport entre les concentrations de micro et macroplastique, peuvent varier d'un bassin versant à l'autre en raison de différences locales dans taux de fragmentation in situ, processus de transport du plastique et niveaux d'émissions de microplastiques primaires (par exemple, granulés de pré-production, microbilles provenant de produits cosmétiques et d'hygiène, poudres à lessive, flocons de peinture et de revêtement).

tu.prop: We acknowledge however, that the extrapolations described above are a limitation of the calibration exercise presented here, as the average mass of river plastic particles, as well as the ratio between micro- and macro-plastic concentrations may vary across catchments due to local differences in in-situ fragmentation rates, plastic transport processes and levels of primary micro-plastic emissions (for example, pre-production pellets, microbeads from cosmetics and hygienic products, laundry powders, paint and coating flakes).
We determined an upper and lower input estimate using equation (1) with respectively k=1.07 10–3, a=1.61 (r2=0.93, n=30) and k=4.46 10−3, a=1.42 (r2=0.91, n=30).

tuv.seg: A sensitivity analysis was conducted by varying the mean ratio micro- to macro-plastic numerical concentration (range: 0.01–0.12) and the average mass of particles (range: 0.002–0.004 g and 0.04–0.33 g for micro- and macro-plastic, respectively) using range values found at sea29.

Une analyse de sensibilité a été réalisée en faisant varier le rapport moyen de la concentration numérique des micro- et macroplastiques (plage : 0,01 à 0,12) et la masse moyenne des particules (plage : 0,002 à 0,004 g et 0,04 à 0,33 g pour les micro et macroplastiques, respectivement) en utilisant les valeurs de plage trouvées en mer.

tu.prop: A sensitivity analysis was conducted by varying the mean ratio micro- to macro-plastic numerical concentration (range: 0.01–0.12) and the average mass of particles (range: 0.002–0.004 g and 0.04–0.33 g for micro- and macro-plastic, respectively) using range values found at sea29.
Further details on the sensitivity analysis are provided in Supplementary Table 1.

tuv.seg: We determined an upper and lower input estimate using equation (1) with respectively k=1.07 10–3, a=1.61 (r2=0.93, n=30) and k=4.46 10−3, a=1.42 (r2=0.91, n=30).

Nous avons déterminé une estimation d'apport supérieure et inférieure à l'aide de l'équation (1) avec respectivement k = 1,07 10–3, a = 1,61 (r2 = 0,93, n = 30) et k = 4,46 10−3, a = 1,42 (r2 = 0,91, n = 30).

tu.prop: We determined an upper and lower input estimate using equation (1) with respectively k=1.07 10–3, a=1.61 (r2=0.93, n=30) and k=4.46 10−3, a=1.42 (r2=0.91, n=30).

tuv.seg: Further details on the sensitivity analysis are provided in Supplementary Table 1.
De plus amples détails sur l'analyse de sensibilité sont fournis dans le tableau supplémentaire n° 1.

tu.prop: Further details on the sensitivity analysis are provided in Supplementary Table 1.

Pour ce qui concerne les eaux de surface :

tu.prop: For the other studies, we computed the daily releases of plastic from rivers into the ocean by multiplying the estimated mass concentrations from observations by the volume of water flowing at the surface layer per day.

tuv.seg: Some studies8,9 directly provide an estimate of daily or yearly plastic mass input rate.
Certaines études [8,9] fournissent directement une estimation du taux quotidien ou annuel d’apport de masse plastique.

tu.prop: Some studies8,9 directly provide an estimate of daily or yearly plastic mass input rate.
For each river, the surface layer thickness was taken at the sampling depth reported by the study, therefore the contribution of any particles suspended below the sampled depth was neglected.

tuv.seg: For the other studies, we computed the daily releases of plastic from rivers into the ocean by multiplying the estimated mass concentrations from observations by the volume of water flowing at the surface layer per day.

Pour les autres études, nous avons calculé les rejets quotidiens de plastique des rivières dans l’océan en multipliant les concentrations massiques estimées à partir des observations par le volume d’eau s’écoulant quotidiennement à la surface.

tu.prop: For the other studies, we computed the daily releases of plastic from rivers into the ocean by multiplying the estimated mass concentrations from observations by the volume of water flowing at the surface layer per day.
We derived the surface layer flow from the river depth and the total monthly averaged discharge predicted by our hydrological model using the month corresponding to the surveyed time period.

tuv.seg: For each river, the surface layer thickness was taken at the sampling depth reported by the study, therefore the contribution of any particles suspended below the sampled depth was neglected.

Pour chaque rivière, l'épaisseur de la couche superficielle a été prise à la profondeur d'échantillonnage rapportée par l'étude, par conséquent la contribution de toutes les particules en suspension sous la profondeur échantillonnée a été négligée.

tu.prop: For each river, the surface layer thickness was taken at the sampling depth reported by the study, therefore the contribution of any particles suspended below the sampled depth was neglected.

tuv.seg: We derived the surface layer flow from the river depth and the total monthly averaged discharge predicted by our hydrological model using the month corresponding to the surveyed time period.

Nous avons dérivé le débit de la couche superficielle à partir de la profondeur de la rivière et du débit mensuel total moyen prévu par notre modèle hydrologique à partir du mois correspondant à la période étudiée.

tu.prop: We derived the surface layer flow from the river depth and the total monthly averaged discharge predicted by our hydrological model using the month corresponding to the surveyed time period.

We combined data on waste generation in kilograms per inhabitant and per day for 182 individual countries5,20 with gridded population densities in inhabitants per km2 (refs 18, 19) to estimate inland MPW production rates per year.

tu.prop: We combined data on waste generation in kilograms per inhabitant and per day for 182 individual countries5,20 with gridded population densities in inhabitants per km2 (refs 18, 19) to estimate inland MPW production rates per year.

tu.prop: In this model, we assumed that inland plastic is accumulated by following natural drainage patterns, derived from the space borne elevation data22.

tuv.seg: The global landmass surface area was divided in river catchments from the U.S. Geological Survey Agency that are used by the Global Land Data Assimilation System (GLDAS, ref.
La superficie mondiale de la masse continentale a été divisée en bassins versants, grâce à la US Geological Survey Agency, qui sont utilisés par le Global Land Data Assimilation System (GLDAS, réf.

tu.prop: The global landmass surface area was divided in river catchments from the U.S. Geological Survey Agency that are used by the Global Land Data Assimilation System (GLDAS, ref.
We used the flow accumulation toolset from ESRI’s ArcGIS software to compute the total mass of inland MPW upstream of the outflow location.
The outflow is the most downstream position in a river catchment and determines the input source point into the ocean.

tuv.seg: We used the flow accumulation toolset from ESRI’s ArcGIS software to compute the total mass of inland MPW upstream of the outflow location.
Nous avons utilisé l'ensemble d'outils d'accumulation de flux du logiciel ArcGIS d'ESRI pour calculer la masse totale des MPW intérieurs en amont de l'emplacement de l'écoulement.

tu.prop: We used the flow accumulation toolset from ESRI’s ArcGIS software to compute the total mass of inland MPW upstream of the outflow location.
Input from catchments with an outflow not connected to the ocean (for example, specifically arid inland areas) were discarded.

tuv.seg: The outflow is the most downstream position in a river catchment and determines the input source point into the ocean.
Le débit sortant est la position la plus en aval dans le bassin versant d’une rivière et détermine le point source d’entrée dans l’océan.

tu.prop: The outflow is the most downstream position in a river catchment and determines the input source point into the ocean.
The model takes into account the presence of artificial barriers and treats them as accumulation sinks, where plastic at the surface is intercepted.

tuv.seg: Input from catchments with an outflow not connected to the ocean (for example, specifically arid inland areas) were discarded.
Les apports provenant de bassins versants dont l'écoulement n'est pas connecté à l'océan (par exemple, spécifiquement les zones intérieures arides) ont été écartés.

tu.prop: Input from catchments with an outflow not connected to the ocean (for example, specifically arid inland areas) were discarded.
As a result, the predicted plastic concentration at the river mouth is representative of the accumulation of inland MPW (in tonnes per year) in the catchment area downstream of artificial dams.

tuv.seg: The model takes into account the presence of artificial barriers and treats them as accumulation sinks, where plastic at the surface is intercepted.
Le modèle prend en compte la présence de barrières artificielles et les considère comme des puits d’accumulation, où le plastique en surface est intercepté.

tu.prop: The model takes into account the presence of artificial barriers and treats them as accumulation sinks, where plastic at the surface is intercepted.
The consideration of dams in our numerical model was motivated by a better correlation found with measurements (Table 4) than when including MPW production rates upstream of dams.

tuv.seg: As a result, the predicted plastic concentration at the river mouth is representative of the accumulation of inland MPW (in tonnes per year) in the catchment area downstream of artificial dams.

En conséquence, la concentration de plastique prévue à l’embouchure de la rivière est représentative de l’accumulation de MPW à l’intérieur des terres (calculé en tonnes par an) dans le bassin versant en aval des barrages artificiels.

tu.prop: As a result, the predicted plastic concentration at the river mouth is representative of the accumulation of inland MPW (in tonnes per year) in the catchment area downstream of artificial dams.

Artificial barriers in rivers may retain 65% of the global input into freshwater, as we calculated an annual 2.13–4.46 million tonnes of plastic introduced upstream of dams that are not accounted as input into the oceans by our model.

tuv.seg: The consideration of dams in our numerical model was motivated by a better correlation found with measurements (Table 4) than when including MPW production rates upstream of dams.

La prise en compte des barrages dans notre modèle numérique a été poussée par une meilleure corrélation trouvée avec les mesures (Tableau 4) qu'en incluant les taux de production de MPW en amont des barrages.

tu.prop: The consideration of dams in our numerical model was motivated by a better correlation found with measurements (Table 4) than when including MPW production rates upstream of dams.
These results were calculated using the parametric equation determined when considering MPW downstream of dams as model proxy.

tuv.seg: Artificial barriers in rivers may retain 65% of the global input into freshwater, as we calculated an annual 2.13–4.46 million tonnes of plastic introduced upstream of dams that are not accounted as input into the oceans by our model.

Les barrières artificielles dans les rivières peuvent retenir 65 % des apports mondiaux en eau douce, puisque nous avons calculé que 2,13 à 4,46 millions de tonnes de plastique sont introduites chaque année en amont des barrages et que notre modèle ne les considère pas comme des apports dans les océans.

tu.prop: Artificial barriers in rivers may retain 65% of the global input into freshwater, as we calculated an annual 2.13–4.46 million tonnes of plastic introduced upstream of dams that are not accounted as input into the oceans by our model.
Including MPW production upstream of dams, when assessing the linear regression would result in different model parameters k and a in equation (1).

tuv.seg: These results were calculated using the parametric equation determined when considering MPW downstream of dams as model proxy.
Ces résultats ont été calculés à l’aide de l’équation paramétrique déterminée en considérant le MPW en aval des barrages comme proxy du modèle.

tu.prop: These results were calculated using the parametric equation determined when considering MPW downstream of dams as model proxy.

While determining regression coefficients based on MPW quantities upstream of dams, our model predicted a global input of 0.76–1.55 million tonnes per year (midpoint at 0.91 million tonnes per year) which remains in the same order of magnitude than the initial scenario.

tuv.seg: Including MPW production upstream of dams, when assessing the linear regression would result in different model parameters k and a in equation (1).
L'inclusion de la production de MPW en amont des barrages, lors de l'évaluation de la régression linéaire, entraînerait des paramètres de modèle différents k et a dans l'équation (1).

tu.prop: Including MPW production upstream of dams, when assessing the linear regression would result in different model parameters k and a in equation (1).
The decrease in predicted global input from the current model may be explained by the number of dams present in the large rivers covered by the observational studies.

tuv.seg: While determining regression coefficients based on MPW quantities upstream of dams, our model predicted a global input of 0.76–1.55 million tonnes per year (midpoint at 0.91 million tonnes per year) which remains in the same order of magnitude than the initial scenario.

Tout en déterminant des coefficients de régression basés sur les quantités de MPW en amont des barrages, notre modèle prédit un apport global de 0,76 à 1,55 million de tonnes par an (point médian à 0,91 million de tonnes par an), ce qui reste du même ordre de grandeur que le scénario initial.

tu.prop: While determining regression coefficients based on MPW quantities upstream of dams, our model predicted a global input of 0.76–1.55 million tonnes per year (midpoint at 0.91 million tonnes per year) which remains in the same order of magnitude than the initial scenario.
In the Yangtze River and Danube River catchments particularly, respectively 68% and 78% of MPW production occurs upstream of a dam.

tuv.seg: The decrease in predicted global input from the current model may be explained by the number of dams present in the large rivers covered by the observational studies.
La diminution des apports globaux prévus par le modèle actuel peut s'expliquer par le nombre de barrages présents dans les grands fleuves couverts par les études d'observation.

tu.prop: The decrease in predicted global input from the current model may be explained by the number of dams present in the large rivers covered by the observational studies.
Therefore, relative MPW mass have less weight on the overall prediction result which ultimately leads in a decrease of our global estimate.

tuv.seg: In the Yangtze River and Danube River catchments particularly, respectively 68% and 78% of MPW production occurs upstream of a dam.
Dans les bassins versants du Yangtsé et du Danube en particulier, respectivement 68 % et 78 % de la production de MPW a lieu en amont d'un barrage.

tu.prop: In the Yangtze River and Danube River catchments particularly, respectively 68% and 78% of MPW production occurs upstream of a dam.
Table 4 Pearson’s product moment correlations of total mass concentration measurements with watershed characteristics.

tuv.seg: Therefore, relative MPW mass have less weight on the overall prediction result which ultimately leads in a decrease of our global estimate.
Par conséquent, la masse relative du MPW a moins de poids sur le résultat global de la prédiction, ce qui entraîne finalement une diminution de notre estimation globale.

tu.prop: Therefore, relative MPW mass have less weight on the overall prediction result which ultimately leads in a decrease of our global estimate.

tuv.seg: Table 4 Pearson’s product moment correlations of total mass concentration measurements with watershed characteristics.
Tableau 4 : Corrélations du moment du produit de Pearson entre les mesures de concentration massique totale et les caractéristiques des bassins versants.

Dam locations were derived from the United Nation Food and Agriculture Organization’s AquaStat dam database23, consisting of 8,800 dams worldwide with a minimum height of 15 m or a reservoir capacity of >3 million m3.

tu.prop: The Global Rivers and Dam Database (GRanD database; ref.

tuv.seg: Dam locations were derived from the United Nation Food and Agriculture Organization’s AquaStat dam database23, consisting of 8,800 dams worldwide with a minimum height of 15 m or a reservoir capacity of >3 million m3.

Les emplacements des barrages ont été dérivés de la base de données sur les barrages AquaStat de l'Organisation des Nations Unies pour l'alimentation et l'agriculture, composée de 8 800 barrages dans le monde avec une hauteur minimale de 15 m ou une capacité de réservoir de > 3 millions de m3.

tu.prop: Dam locations were derived from the United Nation Food and Agriculture Organization’s AquaStat dam database23, consisting of 8,800 dams worldwide with a minimum height of 15 m or a reservoir capacity of >3 million m3.
24) was used for South America as the AquaStat database was incomplete for this continent.

La base de données mondiale sur les rivières et les barrages (base de données GRanD ; réf.

The catchments containing the largest number of dams were the basins of the Mississippi River (718 dams), Yangtze River (342 dams) and Danube River (184 dams).

tuv.seg: 24) was used for South America as the AquaStat database was incomplete for this continent.
24) a été utilisée pour l’Amérique du Sud car la base de données AquaStat était incomplète pour ce continent.

As the analysis is based on natural drainage patterns, the model limitations are that man-made channels are not taken into account and that plastic load accumulates in the main arms of rivers at deltas, introducing uncertainties at local scales.

tuv.seg: The catchments containing the largest number of dams were the basins of the Mississippi River (718 dams), Yangtze River (342 dams) and Danube River (184 dams).
Les bassins versants contenant le plus grand nombre de barrages étaient les bassins du fleuve Mississippi (718 barrages), du fleuve Yangtze (342 barrages) et du Danube (184 barrages).

tu.prop: The catchments containing the largest number of dams were the basins of the Mississippi River (718 dams), Yangtze River (342 dams) and Danube River (184 dams).
These limitations however do not affect the global inputs estimate.

tuv.seg: As the analysis is based on natural drainage patterns, the model limitations are that man-made channels are not taken into account and that plastic load accumulates in the main arms of rivers at deltas, introducing uncertainties at local scales.

Comme l'analyse est basée sur les schémas de drainage naturels, les limites du modèle sont que les canaux artificiels ne sont pas pris en compte et que la charge plastique s'accumule dans les principaux bras des rivières au niveau des deltas, introduisant des incertitudes à l'échelle locale.

tu.prop: As the analysis is based on natural drainage patterns, the model limitations are that man-made channels are not taken into account and that plastic load accumulates in the main arms of rivers at deltas, introducing uncertainties at local scales.
An example for the island of Java in Indonesia illustrating the different datasets involved in this framework is provided in Fig. 5.

tuv.seg: These limitations however do not affect the global inputs estimate.
Cependant, ces limitations n’affectent pas l’estimation des intrants mondiaux.

tu.prop: These limitations however do not affect the global inputs estimate.
